questions.promptHtml: A database containing financial records must encrypt data at rest and in transit with customer-managed keys rotated every 90 days. Keys must be stored in FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated HSM. All key operations must be logged with alerting on unauthorized access attempts.

options.label: Azure SQL Database with TDE using Managed HSM customer-managed key and Key Vault diagnostic logs
Azure SQL Database with service-managed transparent data encryption
Azure SQL Database with Always Encrypted using Key Vault Standard

questions.correctLabel: Azure SQL Database with TDE using Managed HSM customer-managed key and Key Vault diagnostic logs
questions.extraHtml:
SQL TDE with CMK from Managed HSM provides FIPS 140-2 Level 3 encryption at rest. Key Vault diagnostic logs to Log Analytics captures all key operations. Alerts trigger on suspicious access. Service-managed TDE uses Microsoft-managed keys (no customer control). Key Vault Standard is FIPS 140-2 Level 2. Always Encrypted is application-level, not TDE.

Reference: TDE with CMK

questions.promptHtml: A multi-tier application must detect and respond to security threats in real-time including VM compromise, SQL injection attempts, and blob storage anomalies. Security team requires unified alerts, automatic isolation of compromised resources, and integration with SIEM tooling.

options.label: Microsoft Defender for Cloud with Defender for Servers, SQL, and Storage plus Logic App integration
Azure Sentinel for log aggregation
NSG flow logs with Log Analytics

questions.correctLabel: Microsoft Defender for Cloud with Defender for Servers, SQL, and Storage plus Logic App integration
questions.extraHtml:
Defender for Cloud provides workload protection plans detecting threats across VMs, SQL, Storage. Security alerts with severity scoring. Logic Apps for automated response (isolate VMs, SIEM integration). Sentinel for SIEM but Defender detects threats. NSG logs are audit logs, not threat detection.

Reference: Microsoft Defender for Cloud

questions.promptHtml: An organization must ensure compliance with PCI-DSS across 100 subscriptions with continuous compliance monitoring, remediation recommendations, and evidence collection for audits. Solution must provide compliance score and prioritized security recommendations.

options.label: Azure Policy with regulatory compliance built-in initiatives and Defender for Cloud secure score
Manual compliance audits and documentation
Third-party compliance software

questions.correctLabel: Azure Policy with regulatory compliance built-in initiatives and Defender for Cloud secure score
questions.extraHtml:
Azure Policy includes built-in PCI-DSS initiative (50+ policies). Continuous compliance assessment across subscriptions. Defender for Cloud provides secure score, prioritized recommendations, and compliance dashboard. Automatic evidence collection. Manual audits don't scale and lack continuous monitoring. Third-party adds cost and integration complexity.

Reference: PCI-DSS built-in initiative

questions.promptHtml: A storage account with customer data must prevent deletion or modification of blobs for 7 years to meet legal compliance. No administrator, including subscription owners, should be able to delete or modify data during retention period. Solution must support legal hold for litigation.

options.label: Immutable blob storage with time-based retention policy (locked) and legal hold support
Azure Backup with 7-year retention
Blob soft delete with 7-year retention

questions.correctLabel: Immutable blob storage with time-based retention policy (locked) and legal hold support
questions.extraHtml:
Immutable storage with locked retention policy provides WORM (Write Once Read Many) compliance. Even subscription owners cannot delete or modify data until retention expires. Supports legal hold for litigation. Backup is backups, not immutability of source. Soft delete protects against accidental deletion but can be overridden by admins.

Reference: Immutable blob storage

questions.promptHtml: A web application must enforce authentication with multi-factor authentication for external users, certificate-based authentication for internal users, and risk-based sign-in policies denying access when sign-in risk is high. Solution must support integration with on-premises Active Directory.

options.label: Azure AD Conditional Access with MFA, certificate-based auth, and Identity Protection risk policies
Azure AD Connect with password hash sync
Azure AD B2C with custom policies

questions.correctLabel: Azure AD Conditional Access with MFA, certificate-based auth, and Identity Protection risk policies
questions.extraHtml:
Conditional Access enforces authentication methods per user type (external = MFA, internal = certificate). Identity Protection calculates sign-in risk and feeds into Conditional Access for risk-based blocking. Azure AD Connect synchronizes on-premises AD. B2C for customer identity (external), not internal employees.

Reference: Conditional Access

questions.promptHtml: A microservices application requires circuit breaker pattern for API calls, automatic retry with exponential backoff, and request timeout management across 30 services. Solution must handle partial failures gracefully and provide distributed tracing for debugging.

options.label: Azure Service Mesh (Istio) with resilience features and distributed tracing
Application-level retry logic in each service
Azure API Management with retry policies

questions.correctLabel: Azure Service Mesh (Istio) with resilience features and distributed tracing
questions.extraHtml:
Service mesh provides circuit breaker, automatic retries with backoff, timeouts, and distributed tracing out-of-box via sidecar proxies. Configured declaratively, no application code changes. Application-level logic duplicated across 30 services (hard to maintain). APIM for API gateway, not inter-service resilience patterns.

Reference: AKS Service Mesh

questions.promptHtml: An e-commerce system requires command-query separation with write operations to SQL Database and read operations from replicated read-only data stores. Read queries must not impact write performance. Data consistency can tolerate 5-second lag. Solution must auto-scale read replicas based on load.

options.label: Azure SQL Database Hyperscale with read-scale-out replicas
Azure Cosmos DB with eventual consistency
Redis Cache with cache-aside pattern

questions.correctLabel: Azure SQL Database Hyperscale with read-scale-out replicas
questions.extraHtml:
Hyperscale supports up to 4 read replicas with near-real-time replication (<5 sec lag). Read queries routed to replicas (ApplicationIntent=ReadOnly). Zero impact on primary. Replicas scale independently. Cosmos DB requires application refactoring to NoSQL. Redis cache doesn't eliminate database reads (cache misses hit primary).

Reference: Read scale-out

questions.promptHtml: A legacy monolithic application must migrate to microservices incrementally without downtime. Solution must route traffic to either legacy monolith or new microservices based on feature flags during migration period. Both systems must coexist for 6 months.

options.label: Azure API Management with routing policies and feature flags in App Configuration
Deploy microservices, cut over all at once
Use separate domains for monolith and microservices

questions.correctLabel: Azure API Management with routing policies and feature flags in App Configuration
questions.extraHtml:
Strangler Fig pattern: API Management provides facade routing requests to monolith or microservices based on App Configuration feature flags. Migrate feature-by-feature without downtime. After 6 months, decommission monolith. Big-bang cutover risks total failure. Separate domains break existing client integrations.

Reference: Strangler Fig pattern

questions.promptHtml: A containerized application requires external configuration management, feature flags toggleable at runtime without pod restart, and dynamic connection string updates when backend services change. Configuration changes must propagate to 500 pods within 30 seconds.

options.label: App Configuration with Kubernetes provider and feature management
ConfigMaps with manual kubectl updates
Environment variables in deployment manifests

questions.correctLabel: App Configuration with Kubernetes provider and feature management
questions.extraHtml:
App Configuration Kubernetes provider automatically syncs config to ConfigMaps in cluster. Feature flags update dynamically without restart. Apps use App Configuration SDK for 30-second refresh (push notification for faster updates). ConfigMaps require manual updates and pod restarts. Environment variables require pod restart for changes.

Reference: App Configuration with AKS

questions.promptHtml: A data pipeline ingests events from Event Hubs, transforms data with Azure Functions, and writes to multiple destinations (SQL Database, Cosmos DB, Blob Storage). Once an event is successfully processed, it must not be reprocessed even if Function crashes. Solution must guarantee exactly-once processing.

options.label: Durable Functions with checkpointing and idempotent operations
Function with Event Hubs trigger and manual offset management
Logic Apps with built-in retry

questions.correctLabel: Durable Functions with checkpointing and idempotent operations
questions.extraHtml:
Durable Functions maintains state across retries with checkpointing. Idempotent activities ensure duplicate executions don't cause issues. Orchestrator guarantees exactly-once. Manual offset management error-prone (lose offset = reprocess all). Logic Apps built-in retry can cause duplicates without additional idempotency logic.

Reference: Durable Functions

questions.promptHtml: A logging system ingests 1 TB of application logs per day that must be queryable for 90 days. Logs older than 90 days must be archived for 7 years with retrieval within 24 hours for compliance audits. Solution must minimize costs while maintaining query performance.

options.label: Log Analytics with 90-day retention and data export to Storage Archive
Storage Hot tier with lifecycle management to Cool
Cosmos DB with time-to-live and change feed

questions.correctLabel: Log Analytics with 90-day retention and data export to Storage Archive
questions.extraHtml:
Log Analytics provides KQL querying for 90-day hot data. Continuous export sends data to Storage Archive (lowest cost) for 7-year retention. Archive rehydration within 24 hours meets audit requirement. Hot → Cool lifecycle insufficient cost reduction. Cosmos DB expensive for log storage and lacks native export to archive.

Reference: Log Analytics export

questions.promptHtml: An Azure environment with 500 subscriptions requires cross-subscription resource inventory, cost analysis by tags, policy compliance reporting, and security recommendations aggregated in single dashboard. Query results must be exportable to CSV for management review.

options.label: Azure Resource Graph with KQL queries and Azure Workbooks
PowerShell scripts querying each subscription individually
Azure Portal subscription selector

questions.correctLabel: Azure Resource Graph with KQL queries and Azure Workbooks
questions.extraHtml:
Resource Graph enables querying across all subscriptions at scale (seconds vs. hours). KQL supports complex joins, aggregations, and tag-based grouping. Workbooks visualize results with export to CSV. PowerShell serial queries don't scale (500 subscriptions × API calls). Portal subscription selector manual and time-consuming.

Reference: Azure Resource Graph

questions.promptHtml: A deployment pipeline must prevent production deployments that would exceed budget limits. Finance team requires approval for deployments projected to increase monthly costs by more than $10,000. Solution must integrate with CI/CD pipeline and provide cost estimates before deployment.

options.label: Azure Cost Management budget alerts with action groups triggering approval workflow
Manual cost review before each deployment
Azure Advisor cost recommendations

questions.correctLabel: Azure Cost Management budget alerts with action groups triggering approval workflow
questions.extraHtml:
Cost Management forecasts deployment impact. Budget alert triggers when projected spend exceeds threshold. Action group invokes Logic App for approval workflow (email to finance, wait for response, proceed or cancel). Integrated with deployment pipeline. Manual reviews don't scale and delay releases. Advisor provides recommendations, not deployment gates.

Reference: Budget alerts
